title: LogicFlow: The Custom Validation Engine
description: Build a flexible, higher-order validation engine that allows users to create complex rules for data validation. Instead of writing 100 if-statements, you will create a system where validation functions are composed together to validate user profiles.
requirements: A 'createValidator' HOF that takes a rule function and returns a validator that can be applied to a value.
Implement 5 basic rules: isEmail, isMinLength(n), isRequired, isNumber, isUppercase.
A 'combineValidators' function that takes an array of validators and returns a single function that returns TRUE only if all rules pass.
A system to return detailed error messages (e.g., 'Email is invalid' instead of just 'false').
Integration: Apply the final combined validator to a User Registration form object.

milestones.title: Rule Definition
tasks: Ensure rules return a boolean for simplicity in the first pass

milestones.title: HOF Wrapper
tasks: Modify rules to return objects { valid: bool, message: string }

milestones.title: Composition Engine
tasks: Build the combineValidators function using .every() or .reduce()
Create a test suite that validates a complex User object with 5 different fields

stretchGoals: Implement 'async' validators (e.g., checking if a username is taken via a mock API).
Add a 'priority' system where only the first failing rule's error is shown.
Create a UI that highlights the specific field in red based on the validator's output.

evaluationRubric.functionalProgramming: Are the rules pure functions? Is the composition logic clean?
evaluationRubric.extensibility: How easy is it to add a new rule (e.g., isPasswordStrong)?
evaluationRubric.errorHandling: Does the system provide useful, human-readable error messages?
